hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding steric clashes in their more compact pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity but are failing due to poor structural quality. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity will improve the pass rate while maintaining selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the allowed clash score will enforce better structural quality, addressing structural failures and improving pLDDT scores.
Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating a higher number of structurally valid and selective designs.
